label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. The passage starts with the author recalling an incident when he was ten years old, walking with his mother. She stopped to speak to Mr. Lee. The author says he knew he could see Mr. Lee anytime around the neighborhood, so he didn't pay any attention to him. Then after passing Mr. Lee, his mother scolded him for not speaking, using the example of a dog wagging its tail. So, the key part here is that the author didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee when they met. The mother reprimanded him for not speaking. The actual action the author took was not greeting or talking to Mr. Lee. Therefore, the correct answer should be that he ignored him. Looking at the options: (A) He talked with somebody else. But the story says he didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee, not that he was talking to someone else. The mother was talking to Mr. Lee, but the author wasn't involved. (B) He was attracted by a dog. The dog is part of the mother's statement, but there's no mention of a real dog being present when they met Mr. Lee. The dog is a metaphor in her advice. (C) He greeted him politely. No, the author didn't do that. The mother was the one who stopped to speak, but the author didn't participate. (D)
